Precision oncology of SPAtial immune esCape mEchanisms in ovarian cancerSPACE bi-weekly meeting
1Ziqi, Andreas Sep 22rd, 2025

Meeting Agenda•Progress report •WES & bulk RNA-seq on iCAN-DP •Ziqi performed deconvolution analysis on bulk RNA-seq with PRISM •Andreas & Ziqi: iMTB report v2.2.4 generated on 221 samples (with ASCAT for CNA calling) •Andreas ran ASCAT on 221 samples. 5 failed due to invalid tumor purity information (lack of sequencing info on normal tissue) •tCycIF analysis locally •Exploration on RCNs •Diagnoses - iCAN DP

Deconvolution on bulk RNA-seq with PRISM•Reference Dataset 1: EOC dataset provided by Kaiyang •Labels: EOC1-12 (tumor cells), Immune, Stromal

 CCC (Concordance Correlation Coefficient): Extends correlation by also checking whether values are close to the identity line (y = x).

Deconvolution on bulk RNA-seq with PRISM•Reference Dataset 1: EOC dataset provided by Kaiyang •Labels: EOC1-12 (tumor cells), Immune, Stromal

Deconvolution on bulk RNA-seq with PRISM•Reference Dataset 2: scRNA-seq dataset from Inga-Maria’s work on CancerCell •Epithelial cells -> Tumor; Endothelial, Mesothelial, Fibroblasts -> Stromal, Rest -> Immune •29 patients with chemo-naive •The number of cells are quite imbalanced
Tumor gene expression

Deconvolution on bulk RNA-seq with PRISM•Reference Dataset 2: scRNA-seq dataset from Inga-Maria’s work on CancerCell •Epithelial cells -> Tumor; Endothelial, Mesothelial, Fibroblasts -> Stromal, Rest -> Immune •29 patients with chemo-naive •The number of cells are quite imbalanced •First attempt: with all 29 patients, random sample cells, make sure the sum of each cell type are the same

Deconvolution on bulk RNA-seq with PRISM•Reference Dataset 2: scRNA-seq dataset from Inga-Maria’s work on CancerCell •Epithelial cells -> Tumor; Endothelial, Mesothelial, Fibroblasts -> Stromal, Rest -> Immune •29 patients with chemo-naive •The number of cells are quite imbalanced •Second attempt: all cells in 6 selected patients with balanced cell%

Deconvolution on bulk RNA-seq with PRISM
•Reference Dataset 2: scRNA-seq dataset from Inga-Maria’s work on CancerCell •Epithelial cells -> Tumor; Endothelial, Mesothelial, Fibroblasts -> Stromal, Rest -> Immune •29 patients with chemo-naive •The number of cells are quite imbalanced •Second attempt: all cells in 6 selected patients with balanced cell%

Deconvolution on bulk RNA-seq with PRISM•PRISM tend to have a higher estimation of tumor% compared with tumor purity from ASCAT •Tumor purity from ASCAT is quite different compared with the tumor% in tCycIF. •Deconvolution methods tend to return a much more smaller proportion of stromal compared with tCycIF. -> we selected tumor •Should we drop some cell type in the reference? •Are there other ways to improve the results? •Ziqi will meet with Kaiyang tomorrowIn the supplementary material of PRISM manuscript they showed in Fig S6A: How could we know the aberrant and non-aberrant labels in our data?

CNA calling using ASCAT•Here is an example (screenshot taken from iMTB report)

HRP tumor shows enrichment of immune RCN with highest diversity 
•RCN13, RCN3 and RCN9 are significantly enriched in HRP groups. •RCN13 showing the largest log₂ fold change.

HRP tumor shows enrichment of immune RCN with highest diversity 
•RCN13, RCN3 and RCN9 are significantly enriched in HRP groups. •RCN13 showing the largest log₂ fold change. •How does CD8T cells distribute among RCNs? •No significant differences between HRD and HRP tumors.

HRP tumor shows enrichment of immune RCN with highest diversity 
•RCN13, RCN3 and RCN9 are significantly enriched in HRP groups. •RCN13 showing the largest log₂ fold change. •The cellular compositions in RCN13 are no significant differences between HRD and HRP tumors.

HRP tumor shows enrichment of immune RCN with highest diversity
